task.name: Stroop Color-Word Task (RDoC)
reference_literature: Stroop, J. R. (1935). Studies of interference in serial verbal reactions. Journal of Experimental Psychology, 18(6), 643-662.
MacLeod, C. M. (1991). Half a century of research on the Stroop effect: An integrative review. Psychological Bulletin, 109(2), 163-203.

Spieler, D. H., Balota, D. A., & Faust, M. E. (1996). Stroop performance in healthy younger and older adults and in individuals with dementia of the Alzheimer's type. Journal of Experimental Psychology: Human Perception and Performance, 22(2), 461-479.

Steinhauser, M., & Hübner, R. (2009). Distinguishing response conflict and task conflict in the Stroop task: Evidence from ex-Gaussian distribution analysis. Journal of Experimental Psychology: Human Perception and Performance, 35(5), 1398-1412.

Dutilh, G., van Ravenzwaaij, D., Nieuwenhuis, S., van der Maas, H. L. J., Forstmann, B. U., & Wagenmakers, E.-J. (2012). How to measure post-error slowing: A confound and a simple solution. Journal of Mathematical Psychology, 56(3), 208-216.

stimuli.description: Congruent Stroop trial: word and ink color match (e.g., RED in red ink)

detection.selector: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus .stroop-stim'); if(!el) return false; var style = el.getAttribute('style') || ''; var text = el.textContent.trim().toLowerCase(); var colorMatch = style.match(/color:\s*(#[0-9A-Fa-f]+)/); if(!colorMatch) return false; var color = colorMatch[1].toUpperCase(); var map = {'#FF7070':'red','#7070FF':'blue','#70FF70':'green'}; return map[color] === text; })()

response.response_key_js: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus .stroop-stim'); if(!el) return null; var style = el.getAttribute('style') || ''; var colorMatch = style.match(/color:\s*(#[0-9A-Fa-f]+)/); if(!colorMatch) return null; var color = colorMatch[1].toUpperCase(); var map = {'#FF7070': possibleResponses[0][1], '#7070FF': possibleResponses[1][1], '#70FF70': possibleResponses[2][1]}; return map[color] || null; })()

stimuli.description: Incongruent Stroop trial: word and ink color differ (e.g., RED in blue ink)

detection.selector: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus .stroop-stim'); if(!el) return false; var style = el.getAttribute('style') || ''; var text = el.textContent.trim().toLowerCase(); var colorMatch = style.match(/color:\s*(#[0-9A-Fa-f]+)/); if(!colorMatch) return false; var color = colorMatch[1].toUpperCase(); var map = {'#FF7070':'red','#7070FF':'blue','#70FF70':'green'}; return map[color] !== text; })()

response.response_key_js: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus .stroop-stim'); if(!el) return null; var style = el.getAttribute('style') || ''; var colorMatch = style.match(/color:\s*(#[0-9A-Fa-f]+)/); if(!colorMatch) return null; var color = colorMatch[1].toUpperCase(); var map = {'#FF7070': possibleResponses[0][1], '#7070FF': possibleResponses[1][1], '#70FF70': possibleResponses[2][1]}; return map[color] || null; })()

task_specific.color_key_mapping_note: Key mapping is counterbalanced via group_index (default 1). possibleResponses[0] = red, possibleResponses[1] = blue, possibleResponses[2] = green. With group_index=1, this is: comma=red, period=blue, slash=green.

autocorrelation.rationale: Mild lag-1 autocorrelation is consistently observed in speeded response tasks including Stroop. Typical phi values range 0.1-0.3 (Gilden, 2001; Wagenmakers et al., 2004).

fatigue_drift.rationale: Over ~130 trials (practice + 3 test blocks × 40), a small fatigue drift accumulates ~33ms by the end. Consistent with vigilance decrement literature in short tasks.

post_error_slowing.rationale: Post-error slowing is robustly observed in Stroop tasks. Dutilh et al. (2012) report ~50ms PES. We use a range of 30-80ms to capture variability.

condition_repetition.rationale: Condition repetition effects in Stroop reflect priming of the conflict level. Congruency sequence effects (Gratton effect) show ~10-20ms facilitation for repetitions and small cost for switches (Gratton et al., 1992; Egner, 2007).

pink_noise.rationale: 1/f noise is a hallmark of human RT time series (Gilden, 2001; Wagenmakers et al., 2005). Hurst exponents around 0.7-0.8 are typical for choice RT tasks.

post_interrupt_slowing.rationale: No trial interrupt (stop-signal) component in this Stroop task.

between_subject_jitter.rationale: Individual differences in Stroop performance are well-documented. Mean RT SDs of 40-60ms across participants are typical in online samples. The Stroop interference effect (incongruent - congruent) varies across individuals, modeled by rt_condition_sd_ms. Sigma/tau variability captures differences in RT distribution shape. Accuracy variation is modest given the relatively easy congruent condition.

pilot.rationale: Practice block has 4 trials (2 congruent, 2 incongruent), which is sufficient to observe both conditions. One practice block is enough for pilot validation.

phase_detection.test: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus .stroop-stim'); return !!el && typeof expStage !== 'undefined' && expStage === 'test'; })()
phase_detection.practice: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus .stroop-stim'); return !!el && typeof expStage !== 'undefined' && expStage === 'practice'; })()
phase_detection.feedback: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus'); if(!el) return false; var text = el.textContent || ''; return text.includes('Press') && text.includes('enter'); })()

attention_check.response_js: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-attention-check-rdoc-stimulus'); if(!el) return null; var text = el.textContent.toLowerCase(); if(text.includes('press the q key')) return 'q'; if(text.includes('press the p key')) return 'p'; if(text.includes('press the r key')) return 'r'; if(text.includes('press the s key')) return 's'; if(text.includes('press the t key')) return 't'; if(text.includes('press the j key')) return 'j'; if(text.includes('press the k key')) return 'k'; if(text.includes('press the e key')) return 'e'; if(text.includes('press the m key')) return 'm'; if(text.includes('press the i key')) return 'i'; if(text.includes('press the u key')) return 'u'; if(text.includes('first letter')) return 'a'; if(text.includes('second letter')) return 'b'; if(text.includes('third letter') && !text.includes('third to last')) return 'c'; if(text.includes('third to last')) return 'x'; if(text.includes('second to last')) return 'y'; if(text.includes('last letter')) return 'z'; return null; })()
